file_name: AudibleInc_20001113_10-Q_EX-10.32_2599586_EX-10.32_Co-Branding Agreement_ Marketing Agreement_ Investment Distribution Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: "Exclusive Spoken-Word Audio Products" means: (a) spoken-word audio-only products, in a format suitable for digital download and/or streaming, [***]
perturbation.changed_text: "Exclusive Spoken-Word Audio Products" means: (a) spoken-word audio-only products, in a format suitable for digital download and/or streaming,
perturbation.explanation: By removing the [***] (confidential information) from the definition of "Exclusive Spoken-Word Audio Products," the scope of exclusivity becomes uncertain. This omission creates an in-text contradiction if Section 9.1.1, which relies on this definition, refers to specific limitations or products covered by the exclusivity that are no longer clearly defined. The modified definition now lacks specific boundaries, potentially leading to disputes over which products fall under the exclusivity agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 ACSI 9.1.1 Exclusive Spoken-Word Audio Products. During the Term, [***], ACSI will not offer or sell through the ACSI Site, or authorize any third party to sell through the ACSI Site, any Exclusive Spoken-Word Audio Products; [***].
perturbation.justification: The removal of the confidential information [***] from the definition of "Exclusive Spoken-Word Audio Products" introduces ambiguity and thus a contradiction. The redacted definition creates uncertainty in section 9.1.1 regarding the scope of products ACSI is restricted from selling, therefore the change contradicts the original intention to limit product scope in 9.1.1. The original text clearly defined the limitations (even if confidentially). By removing these limitations, it is uncertain what exactly ACSI is allowed to sell, causing a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: "Proration Percentage" means, as of any date within the Refund Period, the following quantity, expressed as a percentage: (a) one (1); minus (b) the sum of: (i) the number of days of the Refund Period which have elapsed prior to such date; divided by (ii) the total number of days in the Refund Period.
perturbation.changed_text: "Proration Percentage" means, as of any date within the Refund Period, a percentage to be determined.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the exact calculation for 'Proration Percentage', the contract introduces uncertainty. The modified text states a percentage will be determined, but fails to specify by whom or how. This directly contradicts the intended clarity of refund calculations mentioned in Section 10.5.1, making the refund amount ambiguous and unenforceable, this creates an in-text contradiction as it now references a percentage that has no clear definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.5 Effect of Termination. 10.5.1 General. Upon termination of this Agreement, each Party in receipt, possession or control of the other Party's intellectual or proprietary property, information and materials (including any Confidential Information) pursuant to this Agreement must return to the other Party (or at the other Party's written request, destroy and certify in writing such destruction) such property, information and materials. In addition, except as provided in Section 10.5.2 [Effect of Termination], Company will promptly upon any termination of this Agreement pay to ACSI a prorated portion of the Annual Fee due for the Year in which termination is effective; provided, however, that if Company terminates this Agreement by reason of ACSI's breach hereof, Company shall have no further payment obligation, and, if such termination occurs at any time during the Refund Source: AUDIBLE INC, 10-Q, 11/13/2000 Period, ACSI shall promptly either (a) refund to Company a percentage of the Annual Fees paid pursuant to Section 5.2.1 [Annual Fees] equal to the Proration Percentage, or, at ACSI's option, (b) cause the transfer and assignment to Company of a percentage of the Shares delivered pursuant to Section 5.3 [Waiver of Payment Obligations] equal to the Proration Percentage. Sections 6 through 8, 10 and 11 will survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a clear calculation for 'Proration Percentage'. The changed text omits this calculation, stating only that the percentage will be 'determined', without specifying how or by whom. This omission directly contradicts Section 10.5.1, which relies on the 'Proration Percentage' to calculate the refund amount due to Company upon termination. The lack of a defined calculation makes the refund provision ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall mutually agree on the nature of such advertising; [***]. With respect to all email advertising, ASCI and Company shall pre-test and plan such advertising in a manner generally consistent with the pre-testing and planning conducted by ACSI and its Affiliates with respect to advertising for other ACSI Product Sub-Sections, with the goal of achieving maximum commercial effectiveness for such advertisements (including, without limitation, by attempting to spread out such advertising in order to not unnecessarily "bunch" the same). [***]
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall mutually agree on the nature of such advertising; With respect to all email advertising, ASCI and Company shall pre-test and plan such advertising in a manner generally consistent with the pre-testing and planning conducted by ACSI and its Affiliates with respect to advertising for other ACSI Product Sub-Sections, with the goal of achieving maximum commercial effectiveness for such advertisements (including, without limitation, by attempting to spread out such advertising in order to not unnecessarily "bunch" the same).
perturbation.explanation: By removing the confidential information [***] from Section 3.2.1, specifically concerning mutually agreed advertising details, it introduces an in-text contradiction because it affects the clarity of the agreement reached by both parties in their advertising campaigns, and it potentially weakens mutual control over the advertising's content and impact. This can later lead to disagreements regarding the appropriateness or effectiveness of the advertising methods employed, contradicting the intent for mutually agreed advertising strategies.
perturbation.justification: The original text indicates mutual agreement on advertising details represented by '[***]', which is removed in the changed text. This removal contradicts the purpose of section 3.2.1, as the original text stipulates the parties should mutually agree on such advertising. The contradicted text, '3.2 Advertising 3.2.1 Amazon.com Customer Base...' outlines specific quantities of emails and product shipments which would implicitly require pre-agreed advertising terms. Without these terms (the omitted '[***]'), the actual implementation of 'Amazon.com-branded e-mails and Amazon.com-branded in-product advertising materials' becomes unclear, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as it removes a previously existing requirement/condition that's relevant in 3.2.1 Amazon.com Customer Base, making the implementation of the advertising campaigns ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.
